title: After-Action Report — The Power Play Rule (the Nickel Fielder)
scope: This is a reference AAR: it documents the mechanic as built, with `file:line` anchors.

rationale.item: The Power Play is the first optional, per-league rule in the engine — a checkbox a league can turn on, the way AL/NL split on the DH for thirty years. It ships off by default, so an existing universe is byte-for-byte unchanged until someone flips the toggle. That makes it a clean A/B lever.
It's a *prevent-defense* lever the way Declared Seconds is a tempo lever — a thing a manager spends at the right moment.

delegation_events.quote: There is no mid-window substitution — it was judged not worth the bookkeeping over a 4-out window, and platooning the nickel by handedness is meaningless since he never bats.

delegation_events.decision: Did not force a 0-PA batting line for the nickel
delegation_events.quote: by design we did *not* force a 0-PA line into the batting table (that would risk the PA↔out reconciliation invariants).

actions.description: Power Play mechanic implemented in one module: deploy decision (maybe_open_window), eligibility (find_nickel), fielding effect (apply_nickel_defense), presence lift (apply_presence_lift), window lifecycle (note_out, clear_window), putout attribution (nickel_putout_for, credit_nickel_putout)
Tuning constants added (POWER_PLAY_* family) at o27/config.py:678-707; auto-exposed as runtime tunables via o27v2/engine_config.py
Box-score Powerplays footer line (format_powerplays_line) rendered under the Seconds line

risk_posture: It ships **off by default**, so an existing universe is byte-for-byte unchanged until someone flips the toggle.

The hard requirement for an optional rule in this engine is that **off = identical**, because games are replayed from stored seeds (`backfill_arc`). Every hook returns **before consuming any RNG** when the rule is off or no window is active.
The **presence lift is below the whole-game noise floor by design** (a ~3% rating nudge over the ~15% of outs a window is open).
this is the one lever the defense has, not a cudgel
treat the *magnitude* as illustrative and the *direction/shape* as the finding

validation_claims.claim: Across a seed sweep, every reproducible seed was byte-identical to the pre-change baseline in both final score and total RNG draw count (a handful of high-event extra-inning games diverge baseline-against-baseline too — pre-existing cross-process nondeterminism)
validation_claims.check_method: Re-run a seed sweep with rule off vs pre-change baseline comparing scores and RNG draw counts

validation_claims.claim: Matched-seed A/B (1,500 games default rates): 1.70 deploys/game, runs Δ −0.78 ± 0.65 (95% CI), 0.40 XBH→single/game, 0.20 hits→out/game; forced every half: 2.74 deploys, −0.65 ± 0.64, 0.60, 0.32
validation_claims.check_method: Re-run the matched-seed A/B harness with synthetic teams

validation_claims.claim: Paired-CRN micro (N=300k): top-of-band +4.4% defense_rating adds ~+0.15pts of outs and shaves ~0.27% of hits through the fielding channel alone, fading to ~nil at the 0.1% floor
validation_claims.check_method: Re-run the paired-CRN micro-benchmark fixing draws while moving the rating

validation_claims.claim: o27/tests/test_power_play.py — 30 cases (off-by-default, eligibility, lifecycle, effect, presence, box-score line)
Regression: the engine, render, declared-seconds, realism-identity, and engine_config suites all pass with the new toggle exposed

non_actions.item: Calibrated season-level impact pass (real seeded league DB, league R/G·H·2B·3B on vs. off)

non_actions.reason: judged not worth the bookkeeping over a 4-out window, and platooning the nickel by handedness is meaningless since he never bats
that would risk the PA↔out reconciliation invariants

residual_risks: Whole-game run delta is noisy (matched-seed RNG streams fork after the first changed outcome) and synthetic teams run hot (~33 R/G).
Pre-existing cross-process nondeterminism from hash()-salted tiebreaks on near-identical synthetic rosters.
open_hooks: Calibrated season-level A/B on a real seeded league DB
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — The Power Play Rule (the Nickel Fielder)
How a league turns it on
Measured impact (does it actually suppress offense?)
unmapped_content: Date written: 2026-05-30; Branch claude/power-play-optional-rule-HmsMs; PR pending. Mechanic reference detail retained in source: nickel fielder NF, scorekeeping position 10, single middle outfielder (F10); window rules (one per defensive half, up to 4 outs, ends with the half, fresh in Declared Seconds, never in super innings, no carryover); full constants table (WINDOW_OUTS 4, XBH_HELD_PROB 0.35, SINGLE_OUT_PROB 0.12, NICKEL_PO_SHARE 0.33, PRESENCE_MIN/MAX 0.001/0.044, ARM_MIN 0.62, FIELD_MIN 0.58, SKIP_GAME_PROB 0.05, MISTIME_PROB 0.09, BLOWOUT_MARGIN 8, DEPLOY_BASE_EARLY/MID/LATE/FORCED 0.03/0.15/0.50/0.90, CLOSE_GAME_MULT 1.4); per-AB polling with hard gates; per-game skip/mistime rolls; deploy-probability ramp with blowout zeroing; eligibility derived from attributes with pitcher wild-card fallback (never one who already appeared); fielding effect conversions and putout attribution; presence lift stash-and-restore LIFO lifecycle layered on leadership flares; box-score line formats including multi-window/multi-nickel forms; 'starting nickel is emergent, not a roster slot'; ephemeral joker-like appearance; 'Things to remember' summary bullets.
parser_notes: Self-described 'reference AAR' documenting the mechanic as built — genre_confidence medium. Date from 'Date written:' line. Dense file:line anchors summarized in unmapped_content.
